title: Drill pipe monitoring and lifetime prediction through simulation based on drilling information
abstract: An apparatus for drilling a borehole in an earth formation includes: a plurality of drill pipes configured to be coupled together to form a drill string that is configured to convey an earth formation disintegrating device while progressing into the earth formation; a communication medium disposed on the drill string and configured to communicate a signal; and one or more processors. The one or more processors are configured to: receive drilling information; determine a stress distribution along at least a part of the drill string using the drilling information; determine a cumulative fatigue damage of at least a part of one drill pipe in the drill string using the stress distribution; and write the cumulative fatigue damage of the at least a part of one drill pipe to a memory.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An apparatus for drilling a borehole (2) in an earth formation, the apparatus characterized by: a plurality of drill pipes (8) configured to be coupled together to form a drill string (9) that is configured to convey an earth formation (4) disintegrating device while progressing into the earth formation; a communication medium (13) disposed on the drill string (9) and configured to communicate a signal; and one or more processors configured to: receive drilling information; determine a stress distribution along at least a part of the drill string (9) using the drilling information; determine a cumulative fatigue damage of at least a part of one drill pipe (8) in the drill string (9) using the stress distribution; and write the cumulative fatigue damage of the at least a part of one drill pipe (8) to a memory. 2. The apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the apparatus further comprises a surface computer processing system (12) and the one or more processors are further configured to write the cumulative fatigue damage to a data base in the surface computer processing system (12). 3. The apparatus according to claim 2, wherein the one or more processors are configured to write an identifier of the at least a part of one drill pipe (8) associated with the cumulative fatigue damage to the data base. 4. The apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the memory is disposed on one or more drill pipes (8) in the plurality of drill pipes (8). 5. The apparatus according to claim 4, wherein the memory disposed on the one or more drill pipes (8) in the plurality of drill pipes (8) is an RFID memory in a radio- frequency identification (RFID) tag. 6. The apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein at least one processor of the one or more processors is located in the drill string (9). 7. The apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the drilling information (4) comprises at least one of a surface torque, an axial load, a rotational speed, a temperature-in, a temperature-out, a pressure-in, a pressure-out, a time, and a depth. 8. The apparatus according to claim 7, wherein the drilling information further comprises at least one of wellbore data and drilling fluid data, the drilling fluid data comprising at least one of a fluid density and a fluid composition, the wellbore data comprising at least one of a wellbore diameter, a wellbore trajectory, and information about whether or not the wellbore contains a casing string. 9. The apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the drilling information comprises at least one of a downhole bending moment, downhole torque, downhole acceleration, downhole axial load, downhole temperature, and downhole pressure on one or more drill pipes (8) in the plurality of drill pipes (8). 10. The apparatus according to claim 9, wherein the processor is further configured to determine temperature induced cumulative aging information using the downhole temperature. 1 1. The apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the processor is further configured to determine whether a pre-selected level of a fatigue limit of the at least a part of one drill pipe is reached using the cumulative fatigue damage for the at least a part of one drill pipe (8). 12. The apparatus of claim 11 , wherein the processor is further configured to send a signal to a user interface warning a user when the cumulative fatigue damage reaches the pre-selected level of a fatigue limit. 13. A method (60) for drilling a borehole (2) in an earth formation, the method (60) characterized by: conveying an earth formation (4) disintegrating device while progressing into the earth formation (4)using a plurality of drill pipes (8) configured to be coupled together to form a drill string (9); communicating a signal using a communication medium (13) disposed on the drill string (9); receiving with one or more processors drilling information; determining with the one or more processors a stress distribution along at least a part of the drill string (9) using the drilling information; determining with the one or more processors a cumulative fatigue damage of at least a part of one drill pipe (8) in the drill string (9) using the stress distribution; and writing with the one or more processors the cumulative fatigue damage of the at least a part of one drill pipe (8) to a memory. 14. The method (60) according to claim 13, wherein the memory is an RFID memory in a radio -frequency identification (RFID) tag disposed on one or more drill pipes (8) in the plurality of drill pipes (8) and the method (60) further comprises reading the memory of the RFID tag (23) using an RFID tag (23) reader when the drill pipe (8) having the RFID tag (23) is retrieved from the borehole. 15. The method (60) according to claim 13, further comprising removing one or more drill pipes (8) of the plurality of drill pipes (8) out of service when the cumulative fatigue damage of the at least a part of one or more drill pipes (8) reaches the pre-selected level of a fatigue limit.
